sections.paragraph:
Ῥοῦφος, ου, ὁ a Latin name found freq. even in its Greek spelling (Diod S 11, 60, 1; 14, 107, 1; Ael. Aristid. 48, 15 K.=24 p. 469 D.; Joseph., index; ins, pap) Rufus.

① son of Simon of Cyrene and brother of Alexander Mk 15:21.

② recipient of a greeting Ro 16:13.

③ a steadfast confessor w. Ignatius and Zosimus Pol 9:1 (cp. Eus., HE 3, 36, 13 and s. Lghtf. ad loc.).—BHHW III 1624. M-M. TW.

freq. freq. = frequent(ly)

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Ael. Aristid. Ael. Aristid. = Aelius Aristides, II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Cyrene Cyrene = text fr. Cyrene

Zosimus Zosimus = Z. alchem, III/IV a.d.—List 5

Pol Pol = Polycarp to the Philippians—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Eus. Eus. = Eusebius of Caesarea, various works; IV a.d.—List 5

Lghtf. Lghtf. = JBLightfoot

ad loc. ad loc. = ad locum (to or at the place under consideration)

BHHW BHHW = Biblisch-historisches Handwörterbuch—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 907
